title: Netskope SWG Web Category Blocking
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="netskope:events" earliest=-7d
| where action="blocked"
| stats count by category, user, policy_name
| sort -count
description: Tracks blocked web requests by URL category across the Netskope SWG — tunes real-time policy and validates acceptable-use enforcement for remote and office users alike.
value: Security teams trend Netskope SWG URL category blocks to detect active phishing campaigns, identify miscategorized domains causing user friction, and measure web policy enforcement effectiveness across the organization.
implementation: Map Netskope URL categories to your acceptable-use policy. Alert when blocks spike for a category after a policy change. Report on top blocked categories weekly for policy review.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily blocks trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Access denied for users in mid-onboarding, role changes pending propagation, or VDI sessions with new device IDs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Netskope web transaction events in `index=netskope` with `sourcetype=netskope:events`. Key fields: `category` (URL category), `action` (Allowed/Blocked/Alerted), `user`, `url`, `domain`, `traffic_type` (CloudApp/Web), `page_site`, `bytes_uploaded`, `bytes_downloaded`.
• Netskope SWG applies URL categorization and filtering inline. Categories include: Adult Content, Gambling, Malware, Phishing, Social Media, Streaming Media, and custom categories.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify web category data:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:events" action=Blocked earliest=-24h
| stats count by category
| sort - count
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Category blocking trends:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:events" action=Blocked earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1d count by category limit=10
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends blocked web categories over time. Spikes in 'Malware' or 'Phishing' indicate active threat campaigns. Steady 'Adult' or 'Gambling' blocks indicate policy enforcement. Spikes in custom categories indicate specific policy changes. A sudden drop in blocks may mean the policy was accidentally relaxed.

Top blocked domains:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:events" action=Blocked earliest=-24h
| stats count dc(user) as users by domain, category
| sort - count
| head 20
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Visit a known-blocked category URL. Verify the block event.
(b) In Netskope: SkopeIT > Page Events > filter by Blocked.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Block trend by category. Row 2 — Top blocked domains. Row 3 — Users with most blocks.
Alert: When Malware/Phishing blocks exceed 2x the weekly average.

Runbook:
1. **Phishing spike** — Active campaign. Check the phishing domain and alert users.
2. **Same domain, many users** — May be a miscategorized domain. Verify and request re-categorization.
3. **One user, many blocks** — Investigate for malware infection or policy bypass attempts.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No block events** — Web policy may be in alert-only mode. Check Netskope Real-time Protection.
• **Category = 'Uncategorized'** — New or obscure domains. May need manual categorization.
grandmaExplanation: Our web security filter blocks dangerous and inappropriate websites. We track which categories get blocked most often, and when phishing or malware blocks suddenly spike, we know there is an active attack and can warn everyone before more people click on dangerous links.
